title: Side-Cage the King
setup: Your kings stand in **opposition** on the e-file. The queen, far on **a1**, has a clean back-rank highway.
hint: Swing the queen to the corner of the back rank — your king already covers {{d7}}, {{e7}} and {{f7}}.
explanation:
## The Idea

A textbook **K+Q vs K back-rank mate** with the kings in opposition. The queen sweeps across the 8th rank from far away.

Play {{Qa8#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{d8}} and {{f8}} — covered by the queen on a8 (8th rank)
- {{d7}}, {{e7}}, {{f7}} — all controlled by your king on **e6**
- The queen is far from the king, so {{Kxa8}} is impossible
- Nothing can interpose on the empty 8th rank

> **Takeaway:** The *cage from the side* is the safest queen mate — no risk of stalemate, queen well-defended by distance, and the kings in clean opposition. Practice this until it's automatic.
